blocked_sources.error: Texas law prohibits HCAD from displaying age-related info ('information indicating the age of a property owner'). Also, React SPA submit does not respond to MCP. Affects Longenbaugh, Fire Safe.
JS-driven SPA — no exposed search route. URL parameters return 404. Affects Mellina, Colleyville, Bridge Street, Altig, NTX Eye Care.
Same SPA engine as Fort Bend — no form submit response to Playwright. Affects Perdue.
JS-driven SPA — no exposed search route. Affects Bellaire (Le's Sugar Land residence).
Partial — first WHITAKER search worked (yielded 627 S Birdsong); subsequent GOLDSTEIN search did not return results despite same form. Likely state corruption between searches.

outcome_assessment.did_playwright_unlock_a_tier_promotions: YES — 6 A-tier promotions vs the 0 from the original run. Hypothesis confirmed.

verification_paths_validated.tx_comptroller_taxable_entity_search: WORKS via JS-driven form on Bexar CAD trueautomation portal; Comptroller search itself works reliably (10/15 entities found; 67% lookup rate). Provides Active/Inactive status + registered agent + officer addresses (the most valuable single deep-dive datum — owner residential addresses for CAD lookup).
verification_paths_validated.cad_ov65_lookup: MIXED. Dallas CAD (classic ASP.NET form on dallascad.org) works well. Bexar CAD (trueautomation) works for owner-name searches and shows OV65 via 'OTHER' exemption + tax-ceiling savings ratio. HCAD blocked by Texas law (and SPA). Tarrant, Fort Bend, Hays CADs blocked by SPA architecture. Travis CAD (not attempted) likely similar.
verification_paths_validated.live_team_page_successor_check: WORKS PERFECTLY via WebFetch — 12/12 = 100% success. Surfaces critical succession signals: family successors with same surname, internal partners already on PIR, Chief of Staff designations indicating completed succession, recently-hired associates as buy-in candidates.

outcome_assessment.promotion_quality: 6 promotions, 3 demotions, 6 holds. Quality is excellent: the 6 promotions all have strong A-tier rationale (solo + active entity + no successor + age indicators present). The 3 demotions identify candidates that the original scoring missed as having succession already complete — these were FALSE-POSITIVE A candidates the deep dive correctly downgraded. Net A-tier count: 6 (vs 0 baseline). This validates the deep-dive layer.
outcome_assessment.highest_value_finding: Donald Perdue / Perdue Insurance Agency: original JSON assumed sole owner; deep dive revealed the Cloud family (3 members including CEO Brandon Cloud) has already taken operational control. Acquisition becomes a 'buy out Perdue residual equity' deal with Cloud-family key-person retention. Completely different deal structure than originally modeled.
outcome_assessment.biggest_surprise: Fire Safe Protection Services: original JSON owner = Stephen McKinney. Comptroller's actual legal owner = Bruce L. Burianek (sole manager of GP entity Sherwood Forest Enterprises LLC). McKinney is operational President, not equity owner. Original L1 anchor was wrong — derived from McKinney's bio, but he's not the seller. L1 downgraded from 73 to 50.

immediate: BUILD a non-Playwright Comptroller search driver (curl + form POST) for production scoring pipeline — works reliably and yields the highest-value data (Active status + registered agent residence).

BUILD a Dallas CAD scraper specifically — DCAD's classic ASP.NET form is the cleanest CAD to drive, and Dallas is a top-3 market for off-market dental/vet/optometry/insurance plays. Worth investing in.
FOR Bexar CAD — use the trueautomation classic form but reset state between searches (warm new navigation each time).
medium_term: INVESTIGATE proxy paths for SPA CADs (Tarrant, Hays, Fort Bend, Travis): (a) check if county has TPADP open data feeds, (b) check if county clerk deed records site has homestead/OV65 indicators, (c) consider a paid data source like DataTree or REalQuest for property records.

FOR HCAD (Harris County) — direct CAD scrape is fundamentally blocked by Texas law. Use proxies: (a) cross-reference homeowner age via voter registration records (Texas voter rolls include DOB and are public), (b) use Harris County Tax Office records (Texas-law restriction is on the *appraisal district* not the tax office), (c) Look up the 'tax ceiling' indicator on Harris County Tax Office's site (taxes.harriscountytx.gov).
do_not_repeat: Don't bother with eSearch.*.com SPAs (Fort Bend, Hays — and likely the same engine on Brazoria, Montgomery, Williamson). They will not respond to MCP form submission. Either skip OR switch to direct county-clerk records.
Don't use HCAD search.hcad.org as the primary verification path — Texas law eliminates OV65 visibility regardless of how well the form drives.
Don't anchor L1 owner-age scoring on 'President / Manager' titles without first cross-checking who actually owns the equity via Comptroller PIR (Fire Safe Protection lesson).
policy_changes_to_scoring_model: When Comptroller PIR officers DIFFER from JSON-stated owner_name, DOWNGRADE confidence to medium until ownership re-verified. Currently we don't reconcile this — it caused the Fire Safe L1 error.

When Comptroller PIR shows 2+ officers/members at DIFFERENT addresses, flag as POTENTIAL CO-OWNERSHIP (likely C_watch, not A) — exit will be internal buyout. Currently treated as A. Lesson from Bellaire and Perdue.

When team page shows founder NOT in roster + Chief of Staff / Lead / Director-of-Operations title held by non-family member, flag as SUCCESSION COMPLETED → C_watch. Currently treated as A. Lesson from Colleyville and Alamo Dog & Cat.
When team page shows founder + 1 recent-grad associate (e.g., Sanchez 2023 at Vision Plus), flag as PLANNED INTERNAL SUCCESSION → B at most, not A. The associate is the future buyer.
